heading: Regulation of credit for reinsurance and reinsurance agreements
label: 8221 Regulation of credit for reinsurance and reinsurance agreements

children.heading: Credit for reinsurance
children.label: a Credit for reinsurance
children.text: If the State of domicile of a ceding insurer is an NAIC-accredited State, or has financial solvency requirements substantially similar to the requirements necessary for NAIC accreditation, and recognizes credit for reinsurance for the insurer’s ceded risk, then no other State may deny such credit for reinsurance.

children.heading: Additional preemption of extraterritorial application of State law
children.label: b Additional preemption of extraterritorial application of State law

children.text: restrict or eliminate the rights of the ceding insurer or the assuming insurer to resolve disputes pursuant to contractual arbitration to the extent such contractual provision is not inconsistent with the provisions of title 9;
require that a certain State’s law shall govern the reinsurance contract, disputes arising from the reinsurance contract, or requirements of the reinsurance contract;
attempt to enforce a reinsurance contract on terms different than those set forth in the reinsurance contract, to the extent that the terms are not inconsistent with this subchapter; or
otherwise apply the laws of the State to reinsurance agreements of ceding insurers not domiciled in that State.
